commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh 1 Chronicles 12:8 And of the Gadites there separated themselves unto David at the stronghold in the wilderness men of might, and men of war fit for the battle, that could handle shield and spear, whose faces were like the faces of lions, and were as swift as the gazelles upon the mountains; Gaddi, a city of the tribe of Gad, (2 Kings xxiii. ) from which many went to join David, when he was in the country of Moab, (Menochius) as others of the Gadites did, ver. A fierce countenance and swiftness were much commended in soldiers. "The eyes are always first conquered. "Primi in omnibus præliis oculi vincuntur, says Tacitus, Mor. (Calmet) See Proverbs xxx. , and 2 Kings ii. (Menochius).
